notes: The engineering layer is consistently additive. The verse names the discipline (titikṣā) with its Sanskrit term and translates it register-faithfully into the engineering domain. The phrase 'this is precisely what titikṣasva looks like under production conditions' frames the engineering scenario as one instance of titikṣā in production, not as the verse's 'real' meaning. The disagreement section preserves the commentarial divergence on mātrā-sparśāḥ (Shankara/Ramanuja etymological paths), which signals to the reader that the verse has its own depth not exhausted by the dashboard-reading analog.
